methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ACSL (Autonomous Systems Company Limited), a publicly listed drone and autonomous systems company trading on the Tokyo exchange, presents a valuation challenge due to the fragmented comparables landscape. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only five identified private peers of relevance—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Terra Drone—with zero public trading comparables in the same vertical. This absence of liquid public benchmarks and the heterogeneity of business models within the sector (urban air mobility, autonomous delivery, cargo operations) limit traditional peer triangulation.

The peer-median methodology proved inapplicable: while four same-sector peers exist, filtering for companies at identical or adjacent development stages or similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for statistical defensibility. The identified private peer set spans divergent funding rounds, geographies, and revenue maturity, preventing reliable median extraction without risking distortion from outliers or stage mismatches.

Given these constraints, a narrowband valuation range cannot be supported by the available data. The analysis highlights a fundamental scarcity: ACSL lacks sufficiently comparable public or private references trading at similar scale and stage. Resolution requires either expansion of the comp set through international drone-sector peers, application of discounted cash flow analysis contingent on revenue visibility, or sector-agnostic SaaS/hardware hybrid benchmarking. Current data does not support a specific valuation conclusion.
